skill.name: deploy
skill.description: Guides agents through local build, development server, browser testing, and test checks before production deployment.
skill.summary: Requires local verification before deployment commands are run.
skill.author: ChakshuGautam

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are true positives because SKILL.md instructs agents to run package scripts and a production Vercel deployment command. The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance findings appear to be false positives; no crypto algorithm or reconnaissance command is present at the cited lines.

medium_findings.title: Deployment Workflow Runs External Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run pnpm build, pnpm dev, pnpm test, and vercel --prod. These commands are legitimate for a deployment skill, but they can execute project scripts and publish changes to production if used without operator approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are directly present at the cited lines and match the skill purpose. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the commands are normal deployment steps.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Finding
low_findings.description: The cited line contains the skill description and does not reference MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak cryptographic algorithm. No evidence found for cryptographic behavior in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line was inspected and contains only frontmatter description text. There is no crypto API, hash algorithm, or encoded cryptographic operation at that location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Finding
low_findings.description: The cited line is a heading for common deployment mistakes and does not ask the agent to enumerate system, network, process, or user information. No evidence found for reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line was inspected and is plain documentation text. Nearby lines describe testing mistakes, not host discovery or environment enumeration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Production Deployment Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes vercel --prod as the final deployment step. This should require explicit user authorization because it can publish live changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The production deployment command is clearly shown in a fenced command block. It is expected for the skill, but it has direct release impact.

content.value_statement: Untested deployments can ship broken builds, failed interactions, or hidden console errors. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a clear local verification checklist before release.
actual_capabilities: Requires a local build before deployment starts.
Directs the agent to start the local development server.
Requires browser testing of changed functionality.
Checks existing test suites before release.
Defines Vercel production deployment as the final step only after verification.
limitations: It does not create or repair tests by itself.
It assumes pnpm and Vercel are the correct project tools.
It does not replace human approval for production deployment.
It does not define rollback or incident response steps.

use_cases.title: Safer Production Releases
use_cases.description: Run a local verification checklist before an AI agent deploys a web application.

use_cases.title: Consistent QA Before Pushes
use_cases.description: Make build, browser, and test checks consistent before code reaches the main branch.

use_cases.title: Agent Deployment Guardrails
use_cases.description: Give coding agents a release gate that reduces accidental untested deployments.

prompt_templates.title: Run the Basic Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the deploy skill to verify this project locally before any deployment command is run.

prompt_templates.title: Check Changed Features
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the deploy skill and focus browser testing on the features changed in this branch.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Release Decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the deploy skill to run build, server, browser, and test checks, then summarize whether deployment is ready.

prompt_templates.title: Gate a Production Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the deploy skill as a strict release gate. Do not run the production deployment command until every required check passes and I approve it.

output_examples.output: Build completed, local server started, changed flows tested in the browser, and tests passed. Deployment is ready after user approval.
Local verification is required first. The agent asks to run the checklist, reports each result, and waits before production deployment.

output_examples.input: Can we push this to production?
output_examples.output: The agent reports the build, browser testing, and test status, then identifies any blocker before release.

best_practices: Require explicit approval before any production deployment command.
Review package scripts before allowing an agent to run them.
Keep browser testing focused on the code changed in the current work.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy only because the build completed.
Do not skip browser testing for interaction changes.
Do not use this skill as a substitute for project-specific release policy.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It makes an agent run local build, server, browser, and test checks before deployment.

faq.question: Does it deploy automatically?
faq.answer: It includes a production Vercel command, but deployment should require explicit user approval.

faq.question: Which projects fit this skill?
faq.answer: It fits JavaScript web projects that use pnpm and deploy through Vercel.

faq.question: Can it find visual bugs?
faq.answer: It can prompt browser testing, but the quality depends on the checks the agent performs.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses normal deployment commands, but those commands can run scripts and publish production changes.

faq.question: Should teams customize it?
faq.answer: Yes. Teams should adapt the checklist to their test commands, hosting provider, and approval process.
